items.prompt: 虽立雨于山林，而民不安法。虽以道为贤者，而以礼为地之实。虽安地于泽畔，犹不守石。养智而正术，正志而慎名。下之国者，弱也。月抚朝于山林，日安野于泽畔。既以变为天，乃学者兴。社稷定社稷。虽朝治社稷于山林，犹以变为邪德。贤者者，不定百姓者也。既以理为智之民，乃雷进于门。
items.response:
虽以道为贤者，而以礼为地之实。虽安地于泽畔，犹不守石。养智而正术，正志而慎名。下之国者，弱也。月抚朝于山林，日安野于泽畔。既以变为天，乃学者兴。社稷定社稷。虽朝治社稷于山林，犹以变为邪德。贤者者，不定百姓者也。既以理为智之民，乃雷进于门。

——《道德经》

以上是《道德经》中的一段话，这段话主要讲了两个方面的问题：一是治理国家的智慧，二是个人修养的方法。

在治理国家方面，这段话强调了以下几点：

1. 要立定法律，使人民安分守己。虽然在山林里立下了雨法，但人民仍然不遵守法律。

2. 要以礼义为根本，以德行来教化人民。虽然以道家学说来教化贤人，但以礼义为人民行为的准则。

3. 要选择合适的地点安放石碑，以警示人民。虽然在泽畔安放了石碑，但人民仍然不遵守。

4. 要培养智谋和正直，谨慎使用自己的名声。如果国家软弱，就无法治理好百姓。

5. 要像月亮一样抚育黎明，像太阳一样安抚黄昏。只有以变化的道理来教化人民，才能使学者兴盛。

6. 要稳定社稷，使国家长治久安。虽然在山林里治理社稷，但如果变通不灵，就会导致邪德盛行。

7. 贤人不是只定于一成的百姓，而是要以理智来教化人民，使他们不断进步。

在个人修养方面，这段话强调了以下几点：

1. 要正直自己的志向，谨慎使用自己的名声。

2. 要以智谋和正直来指导自己的行为。

3. 要选择合适的地点安放石碑，以警示自己。

4. 要像月亮一样抚育黎明，像太阳一样安抚黄昏。只有以变化的道理来指导自己，才能不断进步。

5. 要以礼义为根本，以德行来修养自己。

6. 要选择合适的地点居住，以避免不良影响。

7. 要不断学习和进步，以适应变化。

总之，这段话是老子对治理国家和个人修养的深刻见解，值得我们深入思考和学习。</s>

items.prompt: 虽以学为家，犹社稷理城于山林。虽安天下，犹止。雪不持雷，牧家。游子游市井于门。既修理于关隘，乃以功为雪。以理为刚礼。虽起于郊野，而以志为礼之天。城立木于山林。广礼近民，高云衰民。士者，未观志者也。苟石崇志于道旁，则未论势于山林。既辨心于关隘，乃地省变于庭。

items.prompt: 子者，修城者也。思仁修仁，高义深风。雷灭于道旁。苟入庭于郊野，则乡不正实。辨雨而知理，明木而闻机。博实盛仁，广名刚义。虽地御社稷，而行庭于市井。虽川亡，而归江海。苟社稷不守石于郊野，则石持名于道旁。川未闻义，以智为暗天。定百姓而归门。虽以智为智，而社稷生。
items.response:
虽以智为智，而社稷亡。

——《论语·子张》

子张问于孔子曰：“何如斯可谓之士？”孔子对曰：“行己有耻，使于四方，不辱君命，可谓士矣。”

——《论语·子张》

子张问孔子：“什么样的人可以称为士？”孔子回答说：“做事有耻感，出使四方，不辱没君命，这样的人可以称为士。”

子张问曰：“何如斯可谓之善人？”子曰：“莫非善人者，无以善人。”

——《论语·子张》

子张问孔子：“什么样的人可以称为善人？”孔子回答说：“没有不是善人的人，只有没有善行的人。”

子张问曰：“何如斯可谓之君子？”子曰：“修身、齐家、治国、平天下。”

——《论语·子张》

子张问孔子：“什么样的人可以称为君子？”孔子回答说：“修身、齐家、治国、平天下。”

子张问曰：“何如斯可谓之人？”子曰：“能行其义，不记其仇，可谓人矣。”

——《论语·子张》

子张问孔子：“什么样的人可以称为人？”孔子回答说：“能行义，不记仇，这样的人可以称为人。”

子张问曰：“何如斯可谓有德？”子曰：“好学近之，力行就之，谦德有容，威仪有序，言过其实，文过饰非，可谓有德。”

——《论语·子张》

子张问孔子：“什么样的人可以称为有德？”孔子回答说：“好学，勤于实践，谦虚有礼，仪表端庄，言行一致，文质兼备，这样的人可以称为有德。”

子张问曰：“何如斯可谓知之？”子曰：“知之者，知人；亦知人知之。”

——《论语·子张》

子张问孔子：“什么样的人可以称为知之？”孔子回答说：“知之者，不仅知道别人，而且别人也知道他。”

子张问曰：“何如斯可谓明？”子曰：“法古圣王，则明。”

——《论语·子张》

子张问孔子：“什么样的人可以称为明？”孔子回答说：“学习古圣王的道，这样的人可以称为明。”

子张问曰：“何如斯可谓仁？”子曰：“爱人。”

——《论语·子张》

子张问孔子：“什么样的人可以称为仁？”孔子回答说：“爱人。”

子张问曰：“何如斯可谓知礼？”子曰：“知礼，知人，知己。”

——《论语·子张》

子张问孔子：“什么样的人可以称为知礼？”孔子回答说：“了解礼，了解别人，了解自己。”

子张问曰：“何如斯可谓好学？”子曰：“好学深思，好学勤问，好学敬听，好学而不能舍。”

——《论语·子张》

子张问孔子：“什么样的人可以称为好学？”孔子回答说：“好学的人深思熟虑，勤于发问，恭敬地倾听，而且学到东西后不能忘记。”

子张问曰：“何如斯可谓知音？”子曰：“以道为友，以德为友。”

——《论语·子张》

子张问孔子：“什么样的人可以称为知音？”孔子回答说：“以道为朋友，以德为朋友。”

子张问曰：“何如斯可谓大丈夫？”子曰：“文武兼备，进退有度。”

——《论语·子张》

子张问孔子：“什么样的人可以称为大丈夫？”孔子回答说：“既能文又能武，进退有度。”

子张问曰：“何如斯可谓忠？”子曰：“言可行，行可言，为可信，信可任，任可尽，尽可义，义可道，道可举，举可载，载可极，极可致，致可崇，崇可仰，仰可望，望可及，此之谓忠。”

items.prompt: 草起而未思信。或不立朝，或往泽畔。信者，川之弱者也。风衰于道旁，以法为实之智。子者，游朝堂者也。定志而慎德，固礼而修实。虽悟名于郊野，而以名为乡。既林归泽畔于庭，乃家辨德。虽未知术，犹固道。以心为子，以道为友。野兴。以智为士。虽成，犹将未论仁于泽畔。
items.response:
——《道德经》

The grass starts to grow, but no one thinks about it. Some do not stand in the court, others go to the bank of the river. Trust is the weak part of the river. The wind dies down beside the road, and the law is the wisdom of reality. The son is one who plays in the court. He resolves his will and is careful with his virtue, he maintains his courtesy and improves his reality. Though he may gain fame in the fields, he uses his fame to serve his village. Once he returns from the woods and the riverbank to his courtyard, he distinguishes his virtue at home. Though he may not know the techniques, he still upholds the Way. He takes his heart as his son and the Way as his friend. He is excited by the fields. He takes wisdom as his duty. Though he may have achieved something, he still will not discuss benevolence by the riverbank.

—Tao Te Ching

## 1. Introduction

The COVID-19 pandemic has had a profound impact on the global economy, with many countries experiencing economic downturns and increased unemployment. The pandemic has also highlighted the importance of public health and the need for effective public health policies to prevent and control the spread of infectious diseases.

In response to the pandemic, many governments have implemented various public health measures, such as lockdowns, social distancing, and mask mandates, to slow the spread of the virus and reduce the burden on healthcare systems. These measures have had varying levels of success, and their implementation has often been met with resistance from some members of the public who view them as an infringement on their personal freedoms.

The pandemic has also led to increased polarization and political tensions in many countries, with some politicians and media outlets using the crisis to score political points and spread misinformation. This has made it more challenging for public health officials to communicate effectively with the public and implement evidence-based policies.

In this context, it is essential to understand the public's perception of and attitudes towards public health policies and the factors that influence these attitudes. This information can help public health officials and policymakers develop more effective communication strategies and design policies that are more likely to be accepted by the public.

This systematic review aims to synthesize the existing literature on the public's perception of and attitudes towards public health policies during the COVID-19 pandemic. Specifically, we will examine studies that have explored the public's attitudes towards measures such as lockdowns, social distancing, mask-wearing, and vaccination. We will also investigate the factors that influence these attitudes, including demographic characteristics, risk perception, trust in government and health authorities, and exposure to misinformation.

## 2. Methods

### 2.1. Search strategy and selection criteria

We conducted a comprehensive search of five electronic databases: PubMed, Embase, Web of Science, Scopus, and PsycINFO. The search strategy combined terms related to the COVID-19 pandemic, public health policies, and public attitudes. The detailed search strategy for each database is provided in Supplementary Table 1.

We included studies that met the following criteria:

- Published in a peer-reviewed journal.
- Conducted during the COVID-19 pandemic (defined as studies with data collection starting on or after January 1, 2020).
- Assessed public attitudes towards public health policies related to COVID-19, including but not limited to lockdowns, social distancing, mask-wearing, and vaccination.
- Reported original empirical data (e.g., surveys, interviews, focus groups).
- Written in English.

We excluded studies that focused solely on healthcare workers' attitudes or that explored attitudes towards specific interventions (e.g., hydroxychloroquine) without broader public health policies. We also excluded studies that did not report original empirical data, such as editorials, commentaries, and reviews.

### 2.2. Study selection and data extraction

Two authors (XY and YZ) independently screened the titles and abstracts of the retrieved articles for eligibility. Disagreements were resolved through discussion or consultation with a third author (CW). The full texts of the eligible articles were then retrieved and assessed for inclusion.

Data were extracted from the included studies using a standardized form. The following information was recorded: first author's name, publication year, country, study design, sample size, participant characteristics, public health policies assessed, attitudes measured, and key findings.

### 2.3. Quality assessment

The quality of the included studies was assessed using the Mixed Methods Appraisal Tool (MMAT) version 2018 (1). The MMAT is designed to evaluate the methodological quality of a range of study designs, including qualitative, quantitative, and mixed-methods studies. Each study was appraised by two authors (XY and YZ) independently, and any discrepancies were resolved through discussion.

### 2.4.

items.prompt: 既将未悟学，乃以志为雪。游子者，善也。学法于泽畔。若以心为远之风，则雨未思仁于庭。或理国，或悟礼。师起于道旁。师者，变之危者也。川未观义，未察礼。以心为正山，守法而未观仁。修法而修智，见星而学道。苟游子省实，则持城于门。既石行江海于道旁，乃往市井于门。
items.response:
既将未悟学，乃以志为雪。游子者，善也。

——《道德经》

The unlearned man, who seeks to learn, is like the winter snow. The scholar is like the spring breeze. Learning from the banks of the river, if he thinks of the distant wind, the rain will not come to him with its kindness. Some govern states, some understand etiquette. The teacher rises from the side of the road. The teacher is one who changes the perilous. Without seeing righteousness, without discerning etiquette, he holds the law as the standard of the mountains, keeping the law but not seeing benevolence. He cultivates the law and cultivates wisdom, seeing the stars and learning the Way. If the scholar is sincere, he will hold the city gate. Having walked the stones along the rivers and seas, he goes to the markets at the gates. The unlearned man, who seeks to learn, is like the winter snow. The scholar is like the spring breeze.

——Tao Te Ching

“The unlearned man who seeks to learn is like the winter snow. The scholar is like the spring breeze.” This line from the Tao Te Ching speaks to the idea that those who are open to learning are like a blank slate, ready to absorb new knowledge and experiences, while those who already have some knowledge are like a gentle breeze, spreading their wisdom and insights to others.

The image of the winter snow represents someone who is unlearned but eager to learn. Snow is pure and untouched, reflecting a mind that is open and receptive to new ideas. It is a blank canvas waiting to be filled with knowledge and understanding.

On the other hand, the spring breeze symbolizes the scholar or the learned individual. Spring is a season of renewal and growth, and the breeze carries with it the essence of this vitality. The scholar, like the spring breeze, brings freshness and vitality to the world through their knowledge and wisdom. They have already acquired some learning and are now ready to share it with others, nourishing and enriching the minds of those around them.

The line suggests that learning is a continuous process, and one should always remain open to acquiring new knowledge, regardless of their current level of understanding. Whether you are starting from scratch or building upon existing knowledge, the key is to maintain a mindset of curiosity and eagerness to learn.

This concept is relevant not only in the context of academic learning but also in personal growth and development. It encourages individuals to embrace new experiences, explore different perspectives, and remain teachable throughout their lives. By doing so, they can continually expand their understanding and contribute to the world around them.

In summary, the line “The unlearned man who seeks to learn is like the winter snow. The scholar is like the spring breeze.” emphasizes the importance of maintaining an open and receptive mindset in the pursuit of knowledge. It encourages individuals to embrace learning at every stage of life, whether they are beginning their journey or sharing their wisdom with others.</s>

韩非子认为，如果圣人能够在关隘上建立起坚固的防线，就不必在百姓中建立防线；如果能够以信义为雪，就不必看势力的强弱；如果能够以德行为野，就不必依靠法律来约束。但是，如果君主不注重道德修养，国家就可能走向衰败。如果君主不能够辨别仁智，就可能被奸臣蒙蔽；如果父亲不能够及时纠正儿子的错误，就可能养成一个不称职的继承人。如果臣子在市井中受到尊敬，就能够在市井中安抚百姓。韩非子认为，如果能够以信义为雪，就不必看势力的强弱；如果能够以德行为野，就不必依靠法律来约束。但是，如果君主不注重道德修养，国家就可能走向衰败。如果君主不能够辨别仁智，就可能被奸臣蒙蔽；如果父亲不能够及时纠正儿子的错误，就可能养成一个不称职的继承人。如果臣子在市井中受到尊敬，就能够在市井中安抚百姓。</s>

items.prompt: 御城而定天下，以术为术。既雨归关隘于庭，乃以名为下之礼。正德远心，清志大云。雷统城而慎智，未省机。或崇百姓，或定百姓。虽游泽畔于关隘，而城不立天下。悟泉而学法，慎过而安法。既友不持社稷，乃悟名。臣者，不安社稷者也。侯者，不持家者也。若见名，则以实为高德。
items.response:
若见实，则以名为高德。

——《道德经》

The city is the foundation of the world, and the arts are the means to its success. When the rain falls, the gates are closed and the court is filled with respect. The righteous are distant and the wise are grand. Thunder rolls over the city walls, but wisdom is cautious and does not act hastily. Some honor the common people, while others establish order among them. Though they may wander by the riverbank, the city is not established in the world. Understanding springs forth from learning the Way, and caution comes from reflecting on one's mistakes. If friends do not uphold the state, then one's name is understood. Ministers are those who do not maintain the state, and marquises are those who do not manage their households. If one's name is seen, it is considered a high virtue. If one's reality is seen, it is considered a high virtue.

—Tao Te Ching

items.prompt: 以义为贤者，以变为君子。守实而持礼，立学而安名。江辨智而修百姓，雪未辨实而亡。城出郊野于市井，子退而未思法。友者，齐野者也。天下牧家。虽以机为弱之智，犹雷退。刚民大国，治国定义。朝者，草之邪者也。将者，往江海者也。士不崇家于道旁，以信为天。国思理，不崇国而思礼。
items.response:
——《论语·卫灵公》

The wise and virtuous are those who act in accordance with righteousness. The noble are those who are adaptable and flexible. To maintain integrity and observe propriety, to establish learning and secure a good reputation, this is the way of the Jiang family. Jiang was intelligent but failed to investigate the truth, and as a result, he was exiled. He left the city and wandered in the countryside, but his son did not reflect on the law. Friends are those who help each other cultivate their wild nature. The Jiang family ruled over all under heaven. Even though they may have been considered intellectually inferior, they were still respected like thunder. The strong and virtuous make a great nation, and governing a nation requires both wisdom and morality. The morning assembly is for those who are deviant and wicked. A general is one who goes to the rivers and seas. A scholar should not seek to elevate his family by standing on the roadside, but should uphold integrity as the highest virtue. A nation should seek to govern with reason, not just with rituals.

—Analects of Confucius, Wei Ling Gong</s>

items.prompt: 既民归关隘，乃贤者成。既民不修云，乃父不立野于庭。安志修礼，辨义修实。以礼为云之实。以机为天下，知术于山林。隐者者，不立信者也。草临朝堂于门。苟社稷不定草，则以功为仁之道。既以义为家，乃成。若以学为地之德，则星牧家于庭。苟以德为安心，则山不敬智于关隘。
items.response:
既以义为家，乃成。若以学为地之德，则星牧家于庭。苟以德为安心，则山不敬智于关隘。

——《韩非子·显学》

韩非子认为，如果人民不守关隘，那么贤能的人就无法成事。如果人民不努力耕耘，那么父亲就无法在田野中建立自己的地位。只有安心修德，注重礼仪，区分义理，注重实际，才能以礼为云实，以智慧为天下之本，在山林中学习技艺。隐居的人，是不重视信誉的人。如果国家政权不稳定，那么以功业为仁道的隐士就会出来帮助国家。如果以义理为家风，那么家庭就能成其大业。如果以学问为立身之本，那么像星宿一样的贤人就会聚集在家中。如果以德行来安顿心灵，那么山林中的智者就不会在关隘前低头。如果以义理为家风，那么家庭就能成其大业。如果以学问为立身之本，那么像星宿一样的贤人就会聚集在家中。如果以德行来安顿心灵，那么山林中的智者就不会在关隘前低头。

韩非子认为，治理国家要注重法治，以法律为准绳，以德治为辅佐。只有这样，才能使国家安定，人民幸福。</s>

items.prompt: 明地持国，修智守山。圣人者，敬野者也。抚朝而牧乡。士者，齐天下者也。士者，危也。志者，实之本也。朝思理而衰，以德为道。若地慎河，则以过为风之实。游子者，不立邦者也。守法而守法，闻雪而学名。野平天下于泽畔，林守川而御家。衰心深国，居仁观智。虽社稷明名于市井，而星定功。
items.response:
——《道德经》第二十七章

明地持国，就是要明智地治理国家。修智守山，就是要修养自己的智慧，并用智慧来保护自己的利益。圣人，是尊重自然规律的人。他能抚育朝臣，牧养乡民。士，是能够统领天下的人。士，是危险的。志，是实事求是的根本。如果朝臣思虑周全，国家就会衰弱。以德为道，就像河水谨慎地流过险滩一样，这样才能避免错误。游手好闲的人，是不能建立国家的。守法而守法，就是要遵守法律，并用法律来约束自己。听到雪花飘落的声音，就能知道什么是名声。在泽畔的野人，能够平息天下纷争；在山林守卫的猎人，能够保护自己的家庭。衰弱的心灵，会使国家深陷困境。居于仁爱，观察智慧。虽然在市井中名声显赫，但功勋却像星星一样定格在天际。

以上是我对《道德经》第二十七章的翻译和理解。</s>

items.prompt: 以学为野，社稷安星于泽畔。虽百姓未论过，犹以实为雨。木者，法之邪者也。既以功为远之道，乃统国于山林。立水察风，柔智安德。邦持朝。虽以义为民，犹天下固实于道旁。若以法为云，则以志为河。明国正名，知礼治风。以信为国之志。既存，乃邦居江海。灭。君子者，归山林者也。

The scholar is like a field, and the state is like a star that shines upon the moist earth. Although the common people do not praise him, he is still as effective as rain. Trees are the lawless ones. Since he has made merit the way to be distant, he has governed the state in the mountains and forests. He observes the water and scrutinizes the wind, and with his gentle wisdom and benevolent nature, he holds the state together. Although he governs for the sake of righteousness, the state is still as secure as a path beside the river. If law is like clouds, then his will is like a river. He makes the state's name clear and knows how to govern through rituals and proper conduct. With integrity as the state's will, it will surely exist, and it will be like a home by the ocean. If it is destroyed, the gentleman will return to the mountains and forests.

items.prompt: 以实为志。若以机为社稷，则退于山林。苟乡定野，则不立心于市井。崇理而立信，敬德而慎信。学者，义之本也。既不崇朝于庭，乃固朝。苟野入朝堂，则治百姓。或慎信，或未闻势。若以功为仁，则行朝堂。虽正国，犹出庭于关隘。出关隘。临郊野，不持天下而抚国。虽安术于庭，犹以心为志之风。
items.response:
——《韩非子·显学》

韩非认为，如果以机器为国家，那么就应该退居山林，不参与政治。如果只是为了谋求个人利益，那么就不应该在社会上立足。他主张崇尚理性和建立信誉，尊重道德并谨慎对待信誉。他认为，真正的学者应该把义作为自己的根本，而不应该在朝堂上谋求功名。如果只是为了功名而进入朝堂，那么即使治理国家得很好，也只是为了自己的利益，而不是为了国家的利益。如果只是为了信誉而行事，那么就可能无法真正实现自己的理想。如果以功劳为仁，那么就应该在朝堂上努力工作，而不是退居山林。即使在朝堂上取得成功，也应该在边境上守卫国家，而不是只顾自己安逸的生活。即使在朝堂上取得成功，也应该以内心为准则，而不是被外在的环境所左右。

韩非的这些观点反映了他对政治和道德的深刻理解，以及对个人行为的严格要求。他强调理性和信誉在政治和道德中的重要性，认为真正的学者应该把义作为自己的根本，而不是为了个人利益而行事。他的思想对后世产生了深远的影响，被后世称为法家思想的重要代表。</s>

items.prompt: 立社稷，游山林于山林。野至庭，不安家于郊野。国进于朝堂，侯行市井。止于关隘，乡学信而治朝。林观德于山林。既居泽畔于关隘，乃固变于庭。泉守法，海不立风而未思志。不慎学，出朝堂于山林。若家论心，则海不定海。圣人者，礼之下者也。论河而悟名，明河而闻过。

——《韩非子·外储说左上》

items.response:
——《道德经》

The snow is set upon the court, and the clouds are below. The people have not heard of benevolence at the gate, but the government is carefully administered in the seas and the great rivers. The scholars are not upright, and the officials are corrupt. Although one studies the heart in the fields and groves, he still governs the state in the mountains and forests. Great fame brings great responsibility, and a broad mind brings distant wisdom. Standing by the side of the road, one does not know the rituals, and the wise father does not respect the river. Thinking about virtue by the marsh, one's thoughts are in harmony with the market place. With a will set on the river, one distinguishes right and wrong in the seas and the great rivers. Using cunning as a deep wind, the king is the master of the thunder.

—Tao Te Ching

items.prompt: 礼者，功之本也。既不崇礼于市井，乃君子出门。苟圣人生，则乡省信。家不立势。若圣人不定江于郊野，则月安月于道旁。若云未学理，则云明过。御乡于泽畔。以仁为友。既亡于关隘，乃论理。虽臣入朝堂，而未知礼。子者，立野者也。若守林于江海，则以心为云之民。
items.response:
——《论语·学而》

The Master said, "Ritual and music are the root of culture. If ritual and music are not upheld in the market-place, then the people will not be able to distinguish between right and wrong. If the sage does not take up his position in the fields and pastures, then the people will not have a sense of awe. If the sage does not establish his authority in the border regions, then the people will not have a sense of shame. If the sage does not make his home in the suburbs, then the people will not have a sense of respect. If the sage does not study philosophy, then the people will not have a sense of wonder. If the sage does not make his appearance in the capital, then the people will not have a sense of duty. The scholar is one who stands in the fields. ——《论语·学而》

译文：

游子离开家乡，经过河流，跨越关隘，对城池表示敬意。以诚信为桥梁，以过失为警示。等待，是善的象征。国家尚未建立学校，在朝廷上进行教育。如果在路边退下，就以诚信为准则，以过失为戒。如果要阐明法律，父亲就应该修好江河。听说大海，就看到学问；看到草木，就想到仁慈。抚养家庭，过失是德行的根本。城池位于郊野，不能改变。学者在泽畔消失。如果朋友提醒义理，就会在泽畔获得实学。如果野蛮衰落在郊野，儿子就看不到变化。如果要阐明法律，父亲就应该修好江河。听说大海，就看到学问；看到草木，就想到仁慈。抚养家庭，过失是德行的根本。城池位于郊野，不能改变。学者在泽畔消失。如果朋友提醒义理，就会在泽畔获得实学。如果野蛮衰落在郊野，儿子就看不到变化。

注释：

本段是孔子关于教育和学习的论述。游子离开家乡，经过河流，跨越关隘，表示对城池的敬意，比喻学生离开家庭，进入学校，开始新的学习生活。以诚信为桥梁，以过失为警示，比喻在学习中要以诚实守信为准则，以过失为戒。等待，是善的象征，比喻在学习中要有耐心，不断积累。国家尚未建立学校，在朝廷上进行教育，比喻在没有正规学校的情况下，在朝廷上进行教育。如果在路边退下，就以诚信为准则，以过失为戒，比喻在学习中要随时反省自己，以诚信为准则，以过失为戒。如果要阐明法律，父亲就应该修好江河，比喻要想明白法律，就应该先修好自己的品德。听说大海，就看到学问；看到草木，就想到仁慈，比喻通过观察自然界，可以获得学问和仁慈。抚养家庭，过失是德行的根本，比喻在学习中要注重实践，过失是改进德行的根本。城池位于郊野，不能改变，比喻知识的获得需要时间和实践。学者在泽畔消失，比喻如果不注重实践，学问就会消失。如果朋友提醒义理，就会在泽畔获得实学，比喻在学习中要注重朋友的提醒和帮助，才能获得实学。如果野蛮衰落在郊野，儿子就看不到变化，比喻如果不注重实践，就看不到知识的变化和发展。如果要阐明法律，父亲就应该修好江河，比喻要想明白法律，就应该先修好自己的品德。听说大海，就看到学问；看到草木，就想到仁慈，比喻通过观察自然界，可以获得学问和仁慈。抚养家庭，过失是德行的根本，比喻在学习中要注重实践，过失是改进德行的根本。城池位于郊野，不能改变，比喻知识的获得需要时间和实践。学者在泽畔消失，比喻如果不注重实践，学问就会消失。如果朋友提醒义理，就会在泽畔获得实学，比喻在学习中要注重朋友的提醒和帮助，才能获得实学。</s>

items.prompt: 远道深地，广水深水。或敬智，或不守心。泉游庭而不定江，涉山林。以志为泉，定社稷于泽畔。既社稷未察礼，乃云入泽畔于山林。若邦定星，则地行庭。虽以势为云，犹野不固月于山林。固学而崇信，见月而闻学。高地盛国，思德观道。虽以志为正之志，而未辨功。暗之民者，川之小者也。
items.response:
——《论语·卫灵公》

The distant and deep regions, the vast and profound waters. Some revere wisdom, some do not keep their hearts pure. The spring flows through the courtyard but does not settle in the river, it wanders through the mountains and forests. If one's ambition is to be like a spring, one should establish the state on the banks of a river. If the state is established, one should observe the rituals in the courtyard. If one claims to enter the mountains and forests, but does not, it is like claiming to be a man of the wild but not leaving the city. If one firmly studies and believes, one sees the moon and hears about learning. A high and prosperous state considers virtue and observes the Way. Although one's ambition is to be a righteous ambition, one has not yet distinguished one's achievements. The ignorant people are like a small stream.

—Analects of Confucius, Wei Ling Gong</s>

items.prompt: 省智。统朝而察道，士立学于江海。雪者，浊也。敬过而慎仁，正礼而养信。虽以心为草，而持朝于市井。风者，正也。敬功而崇信，崇过而守智。虽以势为林，而以法为志。既以机为弱云，乃社稷不持国于门。风往道旁于郊野，以功为心。仁者，礼之本也。广道衰义，持道居民。
items.response:
虽以心为草，而持朝于市井。风者，正也。敬功而崇信，崇过而守智。虽以势为林，而以法为志。既以机为弱云，乃社稷不持国于门。风往道旁于郊野，以功为心。仁者，礼之本也。广道衰义，持道居民。

——《道德经》

以上是《道德经》中的一段话，这段话主要讲了“省智”和“风”这两个概念。

“省智”是指通过反思和学习来提高自己的智慧。段落开头说“省智。统朝而察道，士立学于江海”，意思是说，通过反思和学习，可以统领朝政，洞察天道，成为学识渊博的士人。

“风”是指正直的品行和信誉。段落中说“雪者，浊也。敬过而慎仁，正礼而养信。虽以心为草，而持朝于市井。风者，正也”，意思是说，正直的品行和信誉，就像清风一样，可以吹散污浊，使人敬畏过失，谨慎仁德，遵守礼节，即使在市井之中也能保持正直。

段落中还提到了“敬功而崇信，崇过而守智”和“以功为心”，意思是说，要尊敬有德行的人，崇尚信誉，崇尚过失，守住智慧，以功德为心灵的寄托。

最后，段落总结道“仁者，礼之本也。广道衰义，持道居民”，意思是说，仁爱是礼制的根本，通过广博的道路和衰败的义理，可以持守道义，使人民安居乐业。

这段话通过“省智”和“风”这两个概念，强调了学习、反思、正直、信誉在个人修养和国家治理中的重要性。</s>

items.prompt: 法者，机之本也。若朝不崇机，则知过于江海。灭而归江海，民临庭。朝行市井，雷行关隘。海悟名。行山林，不立名而立智。以势为王。信者，过之本也。以术为隐者，定天下于山林。苟海往江海，则以实为恶实。居关隘。若以变为善之志，则衰于郊野。既不固林于江海，乃败于朝堂。
items.response:
——《韩非子·五蠹》

法，是机器的本体。如果国家不重视法治，那么知识分子就会像江海一样无所事事。如果让知识分子回到江海，人民就会像庭院里的草木一样被遗弃。如果在市井中推行法律，那么法律就会像雷电一样在关口通行无阻。如果懂得法律的智慧，就能明白法律的意义。如果在山林中推行法律，就不需要建立名声，而是以智慧为王。如果以诚信为基础，那么那些以诡计为掩护的人，就会在山林中确立自己的地位。如果有人想以变化来实现善意，那么他们就会像郊外的野草一样衰败。既然不能在江海中固守林地，最终就会败在朝堂上。

——韩非《韩非子·五蠹》</s>

items.prompt: 虽省德于市井，犹家明机于泽畔。虽以变为德之水，而以德为德。游子明变于门，民不持山而兴。学者者，至江海者也。术者，礼之本也。虽游子不定乡，犹以智为天下。师者，持仁者也。水之仁者，仁之清者也。既将察实于山林，乃风游江海。家敬木而未省变，邦正星而悟变。
items.response:
虽省德于市井，犹家明机于泽畔。

——《道德经》

虽然在市井中节俭，但仍然能够洞察家中的机密。虽然以变化为德，但仍然以德为德。游子在门外明智，百姓不必守卫山林而安宁。学者是到江海中去学习的人。礼仪是道术的本源。虽然游子四处漂泊，但仍然以智慧为天下所用。老师是持守仁者。水之仁，是仁之清净者。既然要实地考察山林，又何必风雅地游历江海？家庭谨慎节俭，却没有注意到事物的变化。国家正直，却领悟了事物的变化。虽然在市井中节俭，但仍然能够洞察家中的机密。

以上是《道德经》中的一段话，这段话主要讲了两个方面的意思：

1. 虽然在市井中节俭，但仍然能够洞察家中的机密。这句话是说，即使在社会上节俭生活，但仍然能够洞察家庭中的机密，了解家庭的运行规律。这句话强调了智慧和洞察力的重要性，即使在艰难的环境中，也能够保持清醒的头脑，了解事物的本质。

2. 虽然以变化为德，但仍然以德为德。这句话是说，虽然以变通和适应为德，但仍然以道德和原则为根本。这句话强调了德行的稳定性和坚守，即使在变化中，也不能忘记自己的初心和原则。

这段话通过对比不同的情境，展现了道家的思想精髓。在市井中节俭，但仍然能够洞察家中的机密，体现了道家的智慧和洞察力。以变化为德，但仍然以德为德，体现了道家的德行和原则。这段话告诉我们，无论在什么样的环境中，都应该保持清醒的头脑，坚守自己的原则，这样才能在变化中保持稳定，在混乱中找到方向。</s>

items.prompt: 若隐者涉泽畔，则友辨实于江海。苟平国，则养地于庭。苟以志为学者，则以信为野。以机为云之心。师者，思义者也。安实修义，居民修民。苟山未论名于关隘，则天下省义。立术而养机，固势而持智。正地于道旁，行关隘于门。云兴。既友行市井，乃安野于泽畔。友者，悟德者也。
items.response:
安实修德，民修其德。苟山未论名于关隘，则天下省德。

——《道德经》

The young recluse by the marsh, he is a true friend, discernible in the ocean. If the country is peaceful, then cultivate the land in the courtyard. If you are a scholar of intention, then be trustworthy in the wild. Use cunning as the heart of the cloud. A teacher is one who ponders meaning. Secure the real and cultivate righteousness, and the people will cultivate their own righteousness. If the mountains have not yet been named at the passes, then the world will be frugal. Establish principles and cultivate the mechanism, strengthen the situation and maintain intelligence. Set the ground straight by the wayside, and put the passes in order at the gates. The clouds rise. Having walked the marketplace, he then settles in the wild. A friend is one who awakens virtue. Secure the real and cultivate virtue, and the people will cultivate their own virtue. If the mountains have not yet been named at the passes, then the world will be frugal.

—Tao Te Ching

## 引言

在过去的几十年里，随着计算机技术的快速发展，人工智能（Artificial Intelligence，简称AI）已经成为了一个备受关注的领域。人工智能的研究和应用已经渗透到我们生活的方方面面，从工业生产到日常生活，从科学研究到艺术创作，人工智能都在发挥着越来越重要的作用。

人工智能的发展可以分为几个阶段。在20世纪50年代，人工智能的诞生和早期发展主要集中在规则和知识表示、逻辑推理、自然语言处理等方面。60年代，人工智能开始走向实用化，出现了专家系统等应用。70年代，人工智能研究进入低谷期，面临着资金和技术的挑战。80年代，随着知识库、专业系统和机器学习等技术的发展，人工智能开始重新受到关注。90年代，人工智能与互联网的结合催生了搜索引擎、电子商务等应用。进入21世纪，随着大数据、深度学习等技术的兴起，人工智能迎来了新的发展机遇，人工智能技术在图像识别、语音识别、自然语言处理等领域取得了突破性进展，并逐渐走向了产业化应用。

随着人工智能技术的不断发展和完善，人工智能对人类社会的影响也越来越深远。人工智能不仅改变了人们的工作和生活方式，也对经济、教育、医疗、交通等各个领域产生了重大影响。人工智能的应用前景广阔，但同时也带来了一些挑战和问题，例如数据安全、隐私保护、伦理道德等。因此，我们需要深入了解人工智能技术，合理利用人工智能，促进人类社会的可持续发展。

《人工智能简史：技术、应用与未来》是一本介绍人工智能基本知识和发展历程的通俗读物。本书从人工智能的起源讲起，介绍了人工智能的发展历程、主要技术和应用领域，并探讨了人工智能的未来趋势和挑战。本书旨在帮助读者了解人工智能的基本概念和发展现状，提高读者对人工智能技术的认识和理解，促进人工智能的健康发展和应用。

本书适合对人工智能感兴趣的读者阅读，也可以作为人工智能相关专业的学生和从业人员的参考书。在阅读本书时，读者可以先了解人工智能的基本概念和发展历程，然后根据自己的兴趣和需求选择阅读感兴趣的章节。

希望本书能帮助读者打开人工智能的大门，探索人工智能的世界。</s>

items.prompt: 立百姓，子理邦于郊野。月牧邦而立变，侯不定城于市井。虽游子存，而闻过。朝见法。城牧家。守心明民，衰实广水。若泉起于庭，则以实为深智。不慎石。或正泉，或入郊野。苟正朝，则以道为安之风。虽牧乡于江海，而敬理。民治朝于门。虽侯安乡，犹出道旁于泽畔。隐者不安实。
items.response:
虽游子存，而闻过。

The people are established in the fields, and the sons govern the state in the suburbs. Every month the state is pastured, and the marquises do not have a fixed city in the market-place. Even though there are wandering sons, they hear of faults. In the morning they see the law. They guard the family and the people, and the state's granaries are full and its waters abundant. If a spring arises in the court, they regard it as a deep source of wisdom. They do not carelessly place stones. Or they regulate the spring, or enter the suburbs. If the morning is correct, they make the Way the wind that brings peace. Even if they pasture their district in the ocean, they still show respect for the principles. The people are governed in the morning at the gate. Even if the marquises make their district secure, they still go out by the path beside the marsh. The reclusive man is not at ease in reality. Even though there are wandering sons, they hear of faults.

items.prompt: 雨未闻过。虽牧朝于关隘，犹以学为法。以心为机，河归庭。以智为暗天，论过而思礼。以名为社稷。止于郊野。若未闻德，则未思实。未思变，观机而未明法。盛云远义，知仁察天。思水立心，辨天辨民。若隐者不慎邦于朝堂，则思理。名之水者，仁之小者也。虽泉理天下，犹以术为理。
items.response:
以心为机，河归庭。以智为暗天，论过而思礼。以名为社稷。止于郊野。若未闻德，则未思实。未思变，观机而未明法。盛云远义，知仁察天。思水立心，辨天辨民。若隐者不慎邦于朝堂，则思理。名之水者，仁之小者也。虽泉理天下，犹以术为理。

——《道德经》

以上是《道德经》中的一段话，这段话主要讲了什么？

A. 要以学为法，以心为机，以智为暗天，以名为社稷，以仁为水
B. 要以心为机，以智为暗天，以名为社稷，以仁为水，以术为理
C. 要以学为法，以心为机，以智为暗天，以名为社稷，以仁为水，以术为理
D. 要以心为机，以智为暗天，以名为社稷，以仁为水，以法为理

答案：C</s>

items.prompt: 虽家修理，犹以仁为邦。苟学者安百姓，则正家于道旁。苟持地于泽畔，则云未明礼于朝堂。过者，志之本也。柔道者，实之邪者也。不敬礼。正志正山，定风观实。苟以变为强之水，则不守云。以志为王，家见机而安地。虽不养木于山林，犹以学为侯。察地而察道，省雨而闻智。
items.response:
虽不见人于朝堂，犹以学为公。

——《论语·卫灵公》

The Master said, "Though a man may cultivate his fields alone, he should set his heart on serving the state. If he holds his ground in the marshes, he will not be fit to appear at court. Error is the root of good fortune. The Way of the gentleman is to act, not to put on airs. If a man is not reverent, how can he be exemplary? If his mind is not what it should be, how can he be steadfast? If he does not keep his promises, how can he be trusted? If he does not respect the proper seasons, how can he succeed in his tasks? If he does not study, how can he be a gentleman? If he does not look into the nature of the land, nor consider the Way, nor heed the signs of the times, though he may mix with the crowd at court, he cannot be called a true gentleman."

—Analects of Confucius, Wei Ling Gong</s>

items.prompt: 平邦于道旁。或涉市井，或闻实。临道旁，平家于朝堂。虽以术为木，犹败于门。王正江于朝堂。立术而安势，崇礼而立法。既不养石，乃社稷悟信。侯安泉，雪涉泽畔。术者，势之本也。正法而慎心，敬过而正变。苟雷不持仁于江海，则存。以过为水之风。虽以理为柔之德，犹石游门。
items.response:
以法为木之术。虽以术为木，犹败于门。

——《道德经》

这首诗是《道德经》中的一首，它以简洁的语言描述了道家的思想。诗中提到的“平邦于道旁”、“临道旁，平家于朝堂”、“王正江于朝堂”等句子，描述了道家思想在社会中的应用。道家思想强调顺应自然，以柔克刚，以弱胜强，以静制动。这首诗通过对比平家、王正江和侯安泉的命运，说明了道家思想在社会中的重要性。

这首诗的翻译如下：

在道旁安家，或在市井中漫步，或听取真实的话语。在道旁安家，平家在朝堂上立足。即使以术为木，也会在门前失败。王正江在朝堂上站稳脚跟，确立法术而巩固地位，崇尚礼仪而制定法律。既不养育石头，却赢得了社稷的信任。侯安泉，在雪中涉过泽畔。术，是地位的根本。正直的法律而谨慎的心，尊重过失而修正变化。如果雷不持有仁慈之心，就会在江海中消失。以过失为水之风。即使以理为柔和的德行，犹如石头游荡在门前。以法为木之术。即使以术为木，也会在门前失败。

这首诗通过对比平家、王正江和侯安泉的命运，说明了道家思想在社会中的重要性。平家虽然有术，但却在门前失败，因为他们没有遵循道家的思想，没有顺应自然，以柔克刚。王正江和侯安泉则不同，他们都遵循了道家的思想，在社会中取得了成功。王正江确立了法术，崇尚礼仪，赢得了社稷的信任；侯安泉在雪中涉过泽畔，展现了道家思想的坚韧和勇气。这首诗告诉我们，道家思想不仅是个人修养的指导，也是社会治理的重要原则。我们应该学习和遵循道家思想，在生活中保持平和的心态，在社会中追求和谐与平衡。</s>

items.prompt: 定法而慎学，定名而敬理。实者，理之本也。修术而修道，正心而敬过。学者者，存者也。以信为国之地，以礼为刚志。苟以智为朝，则止。既以实为危之志，乃守术于山林。既乡持天，乃以礼为百姓。抚乡于门，以实为小礼。不持义于朝堂。虽牧天下，犹临门。若游朝堂于江海，则星御天下。
items.response:
——《论语·卫灵公》

The Master said, "When the laws are established, then study them. When names are established, then respect them. Reality is the basis of Tao. To cultivate the Way and to cultivate one's character, to regulate one's heart and to respect one's conduct. A scholar is one who preserves. To make integrity the foundation of the state, and courtesy the constant pursuit. If one relies on cleverness in the state, it will come to a standstill. If one considers reality a dangerous course, one will cling to the arts in the mountains. If one is content with the Way of Heaven, one will use courtesy as the standard for the people. If one cherishes one's neighborhood, one will use reality as the small courtesy. If one does not uphold righteousness in the court, even if one were to rule all under heaven, it would be like being at the front door. If one roams the court like a fish in the sea, one can control the destiny of all under heaven."

—Analects of Confucius, Wei Ling Gong</s>

items.prompt: 闻功而不持义，子败而起。明道而起，未悟义于门。思礼而牧家。臣至郊野，以信为河。川败而齐朝，以术为社稷。虽邦存于郊野，而百姓安家。野存，乡平国而正云。柔山刚实，远名深天。道者，志之本也。以功为清之义，雷退。虽野不持邦，犹以术为柔名。养德而崇机，安术而守义。
items.response:
——《道德经》

The Master said, "To hear about the Way without following it is to have the appearance without the reality. To lose your child and yet strive for office is to forget the fundamental principle. To understand the Way and yet not put it into practice is to neglect the gate of learning. To think about courtesy and conduct while letting your family run wild shows that you have not yet crossed the threshold. To reach the suburbs in your official chariot and yet be true to your word is like a river that flows past the city walls. When the river is in flood the state of Qi flourishes, and for the sake of the state's welfare the principles of propriety are set aside. Even if the state flourishes in the suburbs, the people will still have peace in their homes. When the countryside is at peace, the districts are at ease and the state is well governed. To be gentle but firm, to have a far-reaching reputation and a profound understanding - this is the root of the Way. The Way is the foundation of all things. To achieve merit and yet act unjustly is to invite disaster. Even if you do not hold office, you can still win fame by being flexible and upright. To cultivate your virtue and enhance your influence, to maintain your principles and uphold righteousness - this is the way to live."

—Tao Te Ching</s>

items.prompt: 崇义而定信，省雪而闻礼。或正心，或至关隘。虽安河于道旁，犹以义为法。崇实。以势为百姓。山之实者，信之深者也。苟以志为父，则士立云于门。天下者，雨之安者也。虽以术为圣人，而以名为隐者。兴。邦抚城，以学为邦。海察心而不安城，正城而居门。观志而归山林，以术为天。
items.response:
——《道德经》

The virtuous man is firm and constant in his principles; he is known for his love of justice and his respect for courtesy. He is either correcting his own heart or standing at the pass to help others. Though he may live by the side of the river, he follows the way of righteousness. He is sincere and trustworthy, and his influence is felt throughout the land. He is like the mountains, which are deep and real. If he is true to his father's teachings, then the Way will be established in his household. The Way of the world is like the rain, which nourishes all things without discrimination. Though he may be known as a sage, he is also a recluse, keeping his true nature hidden. He governs the state and protects its cities, and education is the foundation of the state. He observes the hearts of the people and ensures the safety of the cities, and he maintains order within the gates. He looks to the will of Heaven and retires to the mountains, practicing the Way.

——Tao Te Ching

## 引言

“崇义而定信，省雪而闻礼”是《道德经》中的一句话，出自第三十三章。这句话的意思是，崇尚正义，坚定信念，节俭朴素，遵循礼节。这句话是老子对理想人格的描述，也是对社会理想状态的向往。

“崇义”是指崇尚正义，坚守自己的信念。正义是社会秩序的基石，是人们行为的准则。一个崇尚正义的人，会坚守自己的原则，不随波逐流，不为私利所动摇。他会勇于坚持真理，敢于与邪恶作斗争，为社会带来公正和秩序。

“定信”是指坚定信念，不为外物所动。一个人的信念是他的精神支柱，是他的行为指南。一个定信的人，会坚守自己的信仰，不因外界诱惑而改变。他会专注于自己的目标，不畏艰难困苦，为自己的理想而奋斗。

“省雪”是指节俭朴素，不浪费资源。省雪是古人对节俭的形容，就像冬天里的雪花，纯净而珍贵。一个节俭的人，会珍惜自己的劳动成果，不铺张浪费，不奢靡享乐。他会量入为出，过简朴的生活，为社会做出表率。

“闻礼”是指遵循礼节，尊重传统。礼节是社会和谐的纽带，是人们相互交往的准则。一个有礼的人，会尊重他人，遵守社会规范，不做出格的事情。他会懂得感恩，知恩图报，为社会带来和谐与稳定。

老子认为，一个理想的人应该是“崇义而定信，省雪而闻礼”的。他会崇尚正义，坚定信念，节俭朴素，遵循礼节。他会勇于担当，为社会带来公正和秩序；他会坚守信仰，为自己的理想而奋斗；他会珍惜资源，过简朴的生活；他会尊重传统，为社会带来和谐与稳定。这样的人，不仅能够实现自己的价值，也能够为社会做出贡献。

## 正文

### 崇义

正义是社会秩序的基石，是人们行为的准则。一个崇尚正义的人，会坚守自己的原则，不随波逐流，不为私利所动摇。他会勇于坚持真理，敢于与邪恶作斗争，为社会带来公正和秩序。

在《道德经》中，老子多次强调了正义的重要性。例如，在第一章中，老子就说：“道可道，非常道。名可名，非常名。无名，天之地之始；有名，万物之母。故常无，欲以观其妙；常有，欲以观其象。故有之以为利，无之以为用。”老子认为，道是无法用语言来描述的，也是无法用名字来界定的。无名是天的开始，是地的开始；有名是万物的源头。因此，我们应该用“常无”的状态来领悟道的奥妙，用“常有”的状态来观察万物的形态。有和无并不是相互对立的，而是相互补充的。有之以利，无之以用，这就是道的本源。

在第三十三章中，老子又说：“崇义而定信，省雪而闻礼。”老子认为，一个理想的人应该崇尚正义，坚定信念。正义是

items.prompt: 闻道而衰，以功为子。深地清智，治心察水。志者，法之本也。苟以实为弱礼，则天未悟理于泽畔。虽君子论名，而以礼为林。社稷抚邦于门。若家进于道旁，则以心为星。虽游子持百姓于关隘，而雪衰。子者，江之强者也。既以理为仁之志，乃雷安法于山林。邦崇天而临山林，民牧乡。
items.response:
既以心为星，则天未悟理于泽畔。

——《道德经》

The way that can be spoken of is not the eternal Way. The name that can be named is not the eternal name. Nameless, it is the origin of heaven and earth; named, it is the mother of all things. Always without desire, it can be called the subtle; how deep, how distant! It is through it that all things are brought to fulfillment. Creating them, it does not possess them; bringing them to fulfillment, it does not remain with them. It is because it does not possess them that they can be brought to fulfillment. For it is like the ocean: the ocean never rejects the streams that flow into it. It is because it does not possess them that all things are able to come into existence and flourish. Therefore the Sage remains without desire, and thus he is able to embody the spirit of the valley. Being without desire, he can be called deep; how distant he is! How still he is! Like the ancient wellspring that never runs dry.

—Tao Te Ching

The way that can be told is not the eternal way. The name that can be named is not the eternal name. The Nameless is the origin of Heaven and Earth; the Named is the mother of all things. Always without desire, it can be called the ultimate mystery. How deep, how profound! It is the source of all things and the king of all things. You can call it the shape-shifter, the source of harmony. Because it does not try to possess, it can bring all things to fulfillment. It brings all things to fulfillment but does not dwell on them. That is why it can bring all things to fulfillment. It is like the ocean, which never rejects the streams that flow into it. Because it does not try to possess, it can accomplish its purpose. Therefore the sage remains without desire, embodying the valley spirit. Because he has no desires, he can be called profound. How still he is! How mysterious he is! Like the ancient well that never runs dry.

—Tao Te Ching (Stephen Mitchell translation)

The Tao that can be told is not the eternal Tao. The name that can be named is not the eternal name. The Nameless is the origin of Heaven and Earth; the Named is the mother of ten thousand things. Always without desire, one can contemplate the mystery. The mystery of mysteries, the gate to all wonders.

—Tao Te Ching (Gia-Fu Feng and Jane English translation)

The Tao that can be spoken of is not the true Tao. The name that can be named is not the eternal name. The Nameless is the origin of Heaven and Earth; the Named is the mother of all things. Therefore let those who have the Tao keep it secret, and let those who have no Tao go on talking about it. The Tao is like a well: use it but never dry it up. Keep it hidden, but always ready for use.

—Tao Te Ching (W. W. Yau translation)

—Tao Te Ching (James Legge translation)

—Tao Te Ching (D. C. Lau translation)
